t_ch: 劳驾您来杂志社里面谈谈？咱们聊一聊赔偿金额吧？
t_en: Would you mind coming into our magazine office to talk? Let's discuss compensation, shall we?

t_ch: 幻太子，你在啊！我请来一位了不得的债主上门哩！
t_en: Hey there, Paraprince! I've brought quite an impressive creditor to visit you!

t_ch: 哔哔，检测到债主电波出现？！我逃——
t_en: Beep beep, debt collector signal detected!? I'm outta here!

t_ch: 别逃哩！是星穹列车的浣熊大人！狸猫和浣熊，一家亲哩！
t_en: Don't run! It's the great Raccoon from the Astral Express! Furbos and raccoons, we're all one big family!

t_ch: 哔啵~亲不亲什么的，不都是来要债的？！
t_en: Beep boop! Family or not, you're all just here to collect debts, aren't you!?

t_ch: 说说吧，怎么赔偿？
t_en: So, what's it gonna be for compensation?

t_ch: 小狸猫，你有什么阴谋？
t_en: What are you plotting, little furbo?

t_ch: 狸猫是犬科！
t_en: You guys are canids!

t_ch: 浣熊大人，社长这个坏家伙抛下大家躲债哩。咱们弟兄几个已经好几个月没发薪水了。
t_en: Great Raccoon, that jerk of a company president ditched everyone to dodge the debts. We brothers haven't gotten paid in months.

t_ch: 看本狸弟兄几个的样子，你也应该明白，就算把咱们卖去做狸火锅也赔不了您几个钱，但是哩——
t_en: As you can see from our sorry state, even if you sold us all for furbo hotpot, you wouldn't get much money back, but...

t_ch: 本狸有一计，咱们可以通狸合作，互惠互狸！毕竟，这家《狸狸周刊》，还是有些销量的！
t_en: I've got a plan! We can work together for some mutual raccoon-furbo benefits! After all, Furbobo Weekly does have quite a readership!

t_ch: 眼下整个二相乐园最炙手可热的新闻，就是星穹列车的到来哩！
t_en: Right now, the hottest news in all of Planarcadia is the arrival of the Astral Express!

t_ch: 只要浣熊大人当上社长，《狸狸周刊》可以为您全面开动，成为列车的专属媒体，输出一大串大热新闻哩！
t_en: Once you become the company president, great Raccoon, Furbobo Weekly can go full throttle for you, becoming the Express's exclusive media outlet, churning out a string of hot scoops!

t_ch: 要不了多久，《狸狸周刊》将成为销量第一的八卦周刊哩！我们不愁没有薪水~浣熊大人也不愁赔偿哩！
t_en: In no time, Furbobo Weekly will be the #1 gossip magazine! We won't have to worry about our salaries! And great Raccoon won't have to worry about compensation!

t_ch: 你让我来给你打白工？
t_en: You want me to work for you for free?

t_ch: 哪儿的话哩！作为您的仰慕者，我们弟兄几个是真心想赔您一大笔精神损失费哩！
t_en: No way, no way! As your devoted fans, me and my bros sincerely want to compensate you handsomely for your emotional distress!

t_ch: …一家杂志社？这事儿有点意思。{NICKNAME}，你觉得呢？
t_en: ...A magazine publisher? Now this is interesting. What do you think, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 我很感兴趣喔。
t_en: I'm very interested.

t_ch: 我就知道你不会错过这样的机会。
t_en: I knew you wouldn't pass up an opportunity like this.

t_ch: 姬子姐也感兴趣吗？
t_en: Is Himeko also interested?

t_ch: 这些狸猫虽然诡计多端，但确实能为咱们提供一些助力：一个为你工作的媒体，一群能到处跑的眼线。在「幻月游戏」中，拥有这样一支后勤团队的好处多多。
t_en: These furbos may be crafty and full of tricks, but they can definitely help us out: A media outlet working for you, and a network of informants who can run around everywhere... Having a logistics team like that during the Phantasmoon Games would come with many perks.

t_ch: 最关键的是，他们免费。
t_en: And best of all, they're free.

t_ch: 说说看，社长要干些什么？
t_en: So tell me, what does a company president have to do?

t_ch: 其实当社长可轻松哩，您跟我来~
t_en: Being a company president is actually super easy, just follow me!

t_ch: 就这么简单？好，现在起，我就是你们的新社长了！
t_en: That's all it takes? Okay, from now on, I'm your new company president!

t_ch: 呜呼~浣熊大人是新社长哩！您交代的任务，弟兄们一定为您办到哩！您还有什么其他交代？
t_en: Woohoo! Great Raccoon is our new company president! Whatever mission you assign, we brothers will get it done for ya! Anything else you'd like us to do?

t_ch: 更改八卦论坛的名字！
t_en: Change the name of the gossip forum!

t_ch: 社长大人觉得「狸狸八卦」不行吗？那想要给它起个什么名字呢？
t_en: Boss, you don't like "Furbobo Gossip"? What name would you prefer then?

t_ch: 月Chat！
t_en: Moon Chat!

t_ch: 围绕幻月游戏大家一起Chat？好名字！
t_en: Chatting about the Phantasmoon Games together? Cool name!

t_ch: 谒神月！
t_en: Death Notes!

t_ch: 本狸突然感到一阵心脏不适哩…但没关系，只要社长喜欢！
t_en: My heart just skipped a beat... but that's okay, as long as the boss is happy!

t_ch: 乐园杂俎！
t_en: Gossip Grove!

t_ch: 乐园砸蛆是什么意思？
t_en: Ghost-Ship Grove!? What's that supposed to mean?

t_ch: 自定义名称3
t_en: Custom Name 3

t_ch: 听起来很有个性的名字！
t_en: That's quite a unique name!

t_ch: 算了，还是用老名字吧。
t_en: Never mind, let's stick with the old name.

t_ch: 您确定吗？
t_en: Are you sure?

t_ch: 就这个了！
t_en: This is the one!

t_ch: 不，让我再想想。
t_en: No, let me think about it.

t_ch: 没什么别的。
t_en: That's all there is to it.

t_ch: 相应的，请你务必要多造些大新闻！
t_en: In return, make sure you create some big news stories!

t_ch: 对了，刚刚那位写您黑稿的{RUBY_B#幻太子}狸狸记者{RUBY_E#}，人事部会将他开除！本社不需要一位对社长不满的员工哩。
t_en: Oh, and that {RUBY_B#Paraprince}journalist furbo{RUBY_E#} who wrote that slander about you? HR will fire him! We don't need an employee around here who's got beef with the company president.

t_ch: 等等，派他去学校潜伏！
t_en: Wait, send him to gather intel at the school!

t_ch: 我听说绘世学院那边有位谒者现身哩，让他为您拍些情报回来吗？
t_en: I heard a Supplicant has appeared at Graphia Academy. Should we have him take some pictures there for you?

t_ch: 没问题，这就安排！本狸这就发布命令，请随时等他传回一手报道哩！
t_en: No problem, I'll arrange it right away! This furbo will issue the order. Stay tuned for his first-hand report!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，看起来你已经很快进入了「社长」这个身份了。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, looks like you're already getting into the groove of being a company president!

t_ch: 这儿的事情办得差不多了。算算时间，丹恒与星期日那边也快到了。方便起见，我会通知他们来杂志社碰头。
t_en: We're pretty much done here, and Dan Heng and Sunday should be arriving soon as well. I'll just have them meet us here at the publishing house.

t_ch: （等待直到同伴到来）
t_en: (Wait until companions arrive)

t_ch: （随便逛逛）
t_en: (Wandering about)

t_ch: 看来这一整栋楼，都成杂志社了……
t_en: It looks like the magazine publisher has taken over this entire building...

t_ch: 莫非…
t_en: Could it be...

t_ch: 严格来说…这一栋楼所在的街道，都是属于我家的地产。
t_en: Strictly speaking... The entire street where this building stands belongs to my family's estate.

t_ch: 据说过去绘世家族全盛期在二相乐园的各处都有财产，只是到了我这一代已经所剩无几了。
t_en: It's said that, in their heyday, the Graphia family owned property all across Planarcadia, but by my generation, very little was left.

t_ch: 等我的路走到尽头时，世界上也许不会再有人能从我手中继承什么了吧？
t_en: When my journey reaches its end, will anyone in this world be able to inherit anything from me?

t_ch: 姬子的意志，我会继承。
t_en: I will inherit Himeko's will.

t_ch: 抱歉，我把话题说得太伤感了。
t_en: Sorry, I made things a bit too heavy there.
Moooom!

t_ch: 傻孩子，说什么浑话。
t_en: Silly child. What nonsense are you talking about.

t_ch: 来吧，去杂志社里瞧瞧，看看这只小狸猫卖什么关子。
t_en: Come, let's go inside the magazine office and see what this little furbo is up to.

t_ch: 这里是姬子家…？
t_en: This is Himeko's home...?

t_ch: 看来，丹恒和星期日还没到。你如果坐不住的话，不妨先下楼逛逛。
t_en: Looks like Dan Heng and Sunday haven't arrived yet. If you're feeling restless, why don't you go outside and explore a bit?

t_ch: （等待直到同伴到来）
t_en: (Wait until companions arrive)

t_ch: （到处逛逛）
t_en: (Look around)

t_ch: 不过片刻，你们就等来了丹恒与星期日。
t_en: After a brief moment, Dan Heng and Sunday arrived.

t_ch: 姬子小姐，我们来了。
t_en: Miss Himeko, we're here.

t_ch: 比预想中慢不少啊——看来有不少收获？
t_en: Slower than expected, huh? Looks like you had quite a harvest?

t_ch: …难说。
t_en: ...That's difficult to say.

t_ch: 见过姬子女士，还有{NICKNAME}也在啊。一别多日，小女子甚是挂念几位。
t_en: Ah, Miss Himeko, and {NICKNAME} is here too! It's been a while. I've missed you all dearly.

t_ch: 停云小姐？这一次是你代表仙舟出使二相乐园吗？
t_en: Miss Tingyun? Are you representing the Xianzhou on this diplomatic mission to Planarcadia?

t_ch: 兹事体大，小女子哪有这样的份量——这趟旅行我只是跟随玉阙仙舟的爻光将军打打杂罢了，在路上恰巧遇见了这二位。
t_en: This is a matter of great importance. A humble girl like me doesn't have that kind of standing. I'm just tagging along with General Yao Guang of the Yuque to help out with odd jobs. I happened to run into these two on the way.

t_ch: 「戎韬将军」…这一路上发生什么事了？
t_en: The Seer Strategist... What happened on this journey?

t_ch: 抱歉，出了些岔子——我不慎戴上了欢愉假面。眼下，我成了幻月游戏中的一名谒者。
t_en: Sorry, there was a bit of a hiccup... I accidentally put on a Mask of Elation. Now, I'm a Supplicant in the Phantasmoon Games.

t_ch: …这条消息，确实有些意外了。
t_en: ...Well, that's quite unexpected.

t_ch: 我知道列车在寻求加入幻月游戏的机会，对此一直有些好奇。抱歉，我是不是让事情节外生枝了？
t_en: I know the Express has been seeking to join the Phantasmoon Games, and I've been quite curious about that. Sorry... Did I make things more complicated than they needed to be?

t_ch: 不必道歉，星期日。你是「开拓」的同伴。没有无名客会阻止同伴寻找自己的道路。
t_en: No need to apologize, Sunday. You are a companion of the Trailblaze. No Nameless would ever stop a companion from finding their own path.

t_ch: 不必道歉，星期日。虽说你并非无名客，但你也是这趟旅程和我们同行的旅伴。没有无名客会阻止同伴寻找自己的道路。
t_en: No need to apologize, Sunday. Though you may not be one of the Nameless, you're still a companion on this journey with us. No Nameless would stop a fellow traveler from finding their own path.

t_ch: …多谢。
t_en: ...Many thanks.

t_ch: 不过，你刚才说…「不慎」？
t_en: But wait... did you just say "accidentally"?

t_ch: 说来话长…恐怕要等爻光将军亲自解释了。
t_en: It's a long story... I'm afraid we'll have to wait for General Yao Guang to explain it herself.

t_ch: 刚才她一直和我们同行，却不知为何不见了。
t_en: She was with us just now, but somehow disappeared without a trace.

t_ch: 爻老板…时常这样，她临时离开，怕是有什么要事吧。
t_en: Madam Yao... Is always like this. She probably disappeared suddenly to deal with something urgent.

t_ch: 这位戎韬将军…和传闻中一样神龙见首不见尾。不如我们等等她？
t_en: This Seer Strategist... Is just as elusive as the rumors say. Should we wait for her?

t_ch: 不必啦，这怎么好意思。因为命数变迁，我不得不暂时延宕几步，以应时现身，请诸位见谅。
t_en: No need. I wouldn't dare impose. The tides of fate shifted. I had to delay my steps to arrive at the proper time. I ask for your understanding.

t_ch: 呀，诸位连我在内正合六爻之数，让我算算……
t_en: Oh my. Counting me, we make exactly six. Let me do some calculations...

t_ch: 近日运势凶危。您可要当心啊。
t_en: Your fortune in the coming days looks grim. You'd better watch out.

t_ch: 吉。今日刚好得偿所愿，合理。
t_en: Weal. Makes sense. You got what you wanted today.

t_ch: 命数凶险。这事儿多半与刃有关了。
t_en: The fates spell danger. This matter is most likely connected to that Blade.

t_ch: 小狐狸否极泰来，吉运常在，是个福将。
t_en: The little fox's misfortune has overflowed into good luck. She's blessed with constant good fortune, a true harbinger of prosperity.

t_ch: 怪了…看不见？
t_en: That's weird... I can't see yours?

t_ch: …所以，你就是符玄说的{NICKNAME}？
t_en: ...So you're that {NICKNAME} Fu Xuan mentioned?

t_ch: 久仰大名，戎韬将军。我是星穹列车的领航员，姬子。有什么事，请和我谈。
t_en: Your reputation precedes you, Seer Strategist. I'm Himeko, Navigator of the Astral Express. Whatever's on your mind, you can discuss it with me.

t_ch: 啊——实在抱歉，卜者遇上命数「无从卜测」之人，难免就有三分激动、三分失态，三分好奇…还有一分敬畏了。
t_en: Ahh, my sincerest apologies. When a diviner encounters someone whose fate is "unfathomable," we can't help but feel three parts excited, three parts flustered, three parts curious... and one part awestruck.

t_ch: 无从卜测…是什么意思？
t_en: What do you mean by... "unfathomable"?

t_ch: 我的「观自在眼」能遍览几位身上的气运，于额顶悬作「吉凶签」。我想以此占卜，献给各位作为见面礼。
t_en: My Eye of Īśvara can scan the aura of fortune surrounding each of you, manifesting as Weal or Woe Signs above your foreheads. I'd like to divine your fortunes as a welcome gift.

t_ch: 但是，这位{NICKNAME}却让我颇为意外…在{F#她}{M#他}的身上，我什么都瞧不见。
t_en: However, this person, {NICKNAME}, really surprised me... I can't see anything from {M#him}{F#her} at all.

t_ch: 对你而言，什么选择都可去试，什么事都可能去做？世上真的有这样的「自在之人」？
t_en: Are you really free to try any choice, to do anything you want? Does an "īśvara" like you actually exist in this world?

t_ch: 我确实能看到选项。
t_en: I can indeed see the options.

t_ch: 对世人而言，人人皆有可选。只是有的选，却未必能逃得脱命数。
t_en: Everyone has options in life. But some options... they can't help you escape your fate.

t_ch: 我见过末王了。
t_en: I've seen Terminus.

t_ch: 你是说，你见证过末王的存在？可这不代表你的人生走进「终末」了呀？
t_en: You mean, you've witnessed the existence of Terminus? But that doesn't mean your life has entered the Finality, does it?

t_ch: 我们星核是这样的。
t_en: That's just how we Stellarons are.

t_ch: 也许你说到点子上了。事涉「星神」，占卜往往诸元错乱，不能成卦。
t_en: You might be onto something. When it comes to "Aeons," divination often goes haywire and no proper reading can be formed.

t_ch: 在将军看来，这意味着什么？
t_en: What do you think this means, general?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>身为自在之人，仅凭入局便能成就「变数」。</color>是好事，也是坏事。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>As an īśvara, their mere presence in the playfield creates a "variable" in the game.</color> It's both a blessing and a curse.

t_ch: 我刚才说了，我们六人的吉凶正合六爻之数——若{NICKNAME}为{RUBY_B#--}凶{RUBY_E#}，则卦象落定于坎☵☵，天险难升，地险难行，凶。
t_en: As I said before, the weal and woe of us six align with the six lines of divination. If {NICKNAME} represents {RUBY_B#--}Woe{RUBY_E#}, then the hexagram falls to Water ☵☵, where dangers lurk above and below, spelling doom.

t_ch: 若{F#她}{M#他}为{RUBY_B#—}吉{RUBY_E#}，则卦象涨落于巽坎☴☵，利涉大川，吉。
t_en: If {F#s}he brings {RUBY_B#—}weal{RUBY_E#}, then the hexagram shifts between Wind and Water ☴☵, presenting a great opportunity to strike. Auspicious.

t_ch: 所以，二相乐园之行的吉凶就在{NICKNAME}的一念之间？
t_en: So, the fate of our journey in Planarcadia rests on {NICKNAME}'s decision?

t_ch: 这责任我可担不起。
t_en: That's way too much responsibility for me to handle.

t_ch: 倒也没有这么玄乎。自在之人虽然拥有远胜凡人的选择权，但依旧身处命数洪流之中。我只想提醒你，慎重选择。
t_en: It's not as mystical as it sounds. While an īśvara has far greater freedom of choice than ordinary people, they're still caught in the currents of fate. I just want to remind you to choose wisely.

t_ch: 我该怎么做？
t_en: What should I do?

t_ch: 我不是自在身，自然也无法指点「自在之人」。
t_en: I am not an īśvara free from fate, so naturally I can't guide those of that disposition.

t_ch: 言归正传吧，您这趟来，想必不只是为我们算卦？
t_en: Back to business. I take it you're not just here to read our fortunes?

t_ch: 来拜会诸位，则是要为之前的「擅作主张」向列车的领航员致歉，并向您借人。
t_en: I'm here to greet everyone, to apologize to the Express Navigator for my previous "indiscretions," and to borrow someone from you.

t_ch: （姬子小姐，你现在明白我是怎么戴上面具了吧？）
t_en: (Miss Himeko, now you understand how I ended up wearing this mask?)

t_ch: （我懂了。她说想借调你行事。我要听听你的想法，如果你不方便拒绝，可以由我来开口——）
t_en: (I get it. She says she wants to borrow you for an operation. I want to hear your thoughts. If you're not comfortable refusing, I can speak up for you...)

t_ch: 两位，还是不要说悄悄话了，这声音大得我都能听见啦！
t_en: You two, no need for whispers. I can hear you loud and clear!

t_ch: 你是怎么…既然将军听见了，能开诚布公说说您这么做的理由吗？
t_en: How did you... Well, since the general heard us, would you mind telling us your reasons for doing this?

t_ch: 我在收集「变数」，此行中我会尽可能肆意妄为，不按常理行事。
t_en: I'm collecting "variables." During this journey, I'll be acting as unpredictably and irrationally as possible.

t_ch: 诸位不必奇怪，帝弓天将或以智勇疆场争胜，或以巧手锻冶兵仗，或以生命坐镇幽冥，而我以卜算为联盟争夺命数，绸缪未来。
t_en: Don't be surprised. Some Arbiter Generals excel in battle with their wit and valor, others master the art of forging weapons, and some guard the netherworld with their lives. I, however, fight for the Alliance by calculating and manipulating fate itself, peeking into the future.

t_ch: 我来，是为磋商「反毁灭同盟」的事宜，之后去往千星城投票，这是正事。
t_en: I'm here to discuss matters regarding the Anti-Destruction Alliance, and then I'll head to Astropolis for the vote. That's the real business.

t_ch: 但按占卜所示，这座乐园世界有着绝非常情的凶险——一巢倾覆，难有完卵。
t_en: But according to the divination, this arcadian world faces an extraordinary danger. When a bird's nest falls, no egg will remain unbroken.

t_ch: …你是说我们所有人会随二相乐园一起覆灭？这又是怎么回事？
t_en: ...Are you saying we'll all perish along with Planarcadia? What's going on here?

t_ch: 情报指出，{RUBY_B#刃}仙舟重犯{RUBY_E#}试图加入幻月游戏——此人身受丰饶令使「倏忽」血肉的侵染，危险至极。
t_en: Intel indicates that a serious {RUBY_B#Blade}Xianzhou criminal{RUBY_E#} is attempting to join the Phantasmoon Games. This individual has been infected by the flesh of Shuhu, the Emanator of the Abundance, and is extremely dangerous.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>依照本座解读：他压抑已久的凶性终将难以遏制，会为所有人带来灭顶之灾。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>According to my interpretation, his long-suppressed violent nature will eventually become uncontrollable, bringing catastrophic disaster to everyone.</color>

t_ch: …他不可能做到。
t_en: ...There's no way he could do that.

t_ch: 占卜显示如此，比起问「他能不能」，倒不如问「他如何做到」？
t_en: That's the divination's answer. Rather than asking "whether he could," we should ask, "How would he do it?"

t_ch: 我占得「龙战于野，其血玄黄」一爻。「龙战」一词固然能解作「鏖战」，但是否预示着你和刃的相争酿成大难？
t_en: My divination shows "Dragons clash in the wilderness, their ichor dark and yellow." While the term "dragon clash" could simply mean "fierce battle," could it be foreshadowing a great calamity from your conflict with Blade?

t_ch: 也可能，这命数另有其他解读…不过眼下我所得的情报有限，只好暂且先将刃这个最容易生变的危险因素摆在最重要的位置考虑。
t_en: Perhaps this prophecy has other interpretations... But with my limited intel right now, I have to prioritize Blade, this most volatile and dangerous factor.

t_ch: 我听人说，对预言的不同理解会导致截然相反的结果。在一切有眉目前，还是存而不论为好。
t_en: I've heard that different interpretations of prophecies can lead to completely opposite outcomes. Until things become clearer, it's better to reserve judgment.

t_ch: 存而不论，好。无论如何，此行我携来了一样「刃亲手所锻的造物」，若事情到了难以收拾的地步，也许可以暂时恢复其心智，争取片刻的胜机。
t_en: Let's put that aside for now. In any case, I've brought along a "creation forged by Blade himself." If things get out of hand, it might temporarily restore his sanity, giving us a brief chance at victory.

t_ch: …是哪一样？
t_en: ...What is it?

t_ch: 抱歉，命数再度生变，我…必须离开了。
t_en: I'm sorry, but fate has shifted once again... I must take my leave.

t_ch: 为了让列车诸位放心，我可以指天信誓，星期日先生不必为仙舟尽任何义务，相反，我将为他提供使团力所能及的一切协助——
t_en: To put everyone on the Express at ease, I solemnly swear that Mr. Sunday won't be obligated to do anything for the Xianzhou. On the contrary, our delegation will give him every ounce of aid within our power...

t_ch: 将军，你说什么？
t_en: What did you say, general?

t_ch: …「力所能及的一切协助」？她消失了。
t_en: ..."Aid within our power?" She... She's gone.

t_ch: 呀，这么快就…爻老板看来是忙得很呀。
t_en: Whoa, that was quick... Madam Yao must be really busy.

t_ch: 此前她也是这样凭空消失的吗？
t_en: Has she vanished into thin air like this before?

t_ch: 不错，具体内情我也不太清楚。这在爻老板身上是常事，也不知多久才能回来——她不是有心而为，我代她向各位致歉。
t_en: Yes... I don't know the details myself. With Madam Yao it often happens, and no one knows when she'll return. She doesn't do it on purpose. Allow me to apologize on her behalf.

t_ch: 无妨，至少我们知道了仙舟的来意。
t_en: No worries, at least we know what the Xianzhou is up to.

t_ch: 眼下，列车有两位同伴仍被困在某处，我和{NICKNAME}必须动身去「酒馆」查清一些线索。
t_en: Right now, two companions from the Express are still trapped somewhere. {NICKNAME} and I must head to the tavern to uncover some clues.

t_ch: 需要小女子帮忙的话，姬子小姐请尽管开口。
t_en: If Miss Himeko needs my help, please don't hesitate to ask.

t_ch: 是三月和瓦尔特先生出了事？我同你们一起去。
t_en: Are March and Mr. Yang in trouble? I'll go with you.

t_ch: 不，你和星期日随那位戎韬将军一起行动——眼下还没到非搬救兵不可的时候。
t_en: No, you and Sunday will act together with the Seer Strategist. We're not at the point where we need to call in reinforcements yet.

t_ch: 刃的事情不同寻常，那位戎韬将军的顾虑绝不是空穴来风的担忧。
t_en: The situation with Blade is unusual. That Seer Strategist's concerns aren't just baseless worries.

t_ch: 这也是我的请求，我不想看到这场幻月游戏为故乡带来灭顶之灾，用那位将军的话说：请你们…尽力带来「变数」吧！
t_en: I don't want to see the Phantasmoon Games bring catastrophe to my homeland, so this is also my request. To put it in the general's words: Please do your best to introduce new "variables"!

t_ch: 我明白姬子小姐的用心。{NICKNAME}，三月和瓦尔特先生就拜托你了。
t_en: I understand Miss Himeko's intentions. {NICKNAME}, I'll leave March and Mr. Yang in your care.

t_ch: 关于刃…
t_en: About Blade...

t_ch: …刃的出现，意味着星核猎手在这个世界也有所行动。
t_en: ...Blade's appearance means the Stellaron Hunters are also on the move in this world.

t_ch: 以此前的种种迹象看来，这场二相乐园之行也至关重要。
t_en: From everything we've seen, this mission to Planarcadia is of the utmost importance.

t_ch: 我会跟随爻光将军一同，查清星核猎手的布局。希望这一次无论发生什么，都不会危及你们…我会尽力确保这一点。
t_en: I will accompany General Yao Guang to uncover the Stellaron Hunters' schemes. Whatever happens this time, I hope it won't put any of you in danger... I'll do my best to make sure of that.

t_ch: 关于三月和瓦尔特…
t_en: About March and Welt...

t_ch: 什么…他们俩被困在了学校里，被迫「成为」了学生和老师？治安官也对这些事情不闻不问？
t_en: What... Those two are trapped in the school, forced to "become" a student and teacher? And the security officers are turning a blind eye to all this?

t_ch: 虽然三月和瓦尔特先生听起来暂时没有性命之忧，但谁也不知道火花想做什么。未免夜长梦多，还是尽快将他们解救出来吧。
t_en: Though March and Mr. Yang don't seem in mortal danger for now, who knows what Sparxie is plotting. The longer we delay, the worse it could get... Better to rescue them as soon as possible.

t_ch: 「幻月游戏」…真是个奇怪的仪式。对此地的居民来说，这样的「欢愉」到底意味着什么？阿哈为什么要推行这样的游戏？
t_en: The Phantasmoon Games... What a bizarre ceremony. What does this type of Elation really mean for the people of this place? Why would Aha promote such a game?

t_ch: 放心吧，这里还有我呢。
t_en: Don't worry. I'm here with you.

t_ch: 无论发生什么，记得告诉我进展。
t_en: Whatever happens, keep me posted on how things go.

t_ch: 见过恩公。匹诺康尼至此，已是一别多日，小女子可是日夜挂念您呐。
t_en: Greetings, benefactor. It's been many days since we met in Penacony, and I've been thinking of you day and night.

t_ch: 停云，好久不见了。
t_en: Tingyun... it's been a while.

t_ch: 你最近还好吗？
t_en: How are things these days?

t_ch: 感谢您的关心，小女子身体无恙。
t_en: Thank you for your concern. I'm doing well.

t_ch: 虽然停云本事低微，但好在这位爻老板夸我有杀贼之心，正是她想要的「变数」。故此，这趟出行她调了我来寄在帐下听令。这不，就和您再续前缘了嘛！
t_en: Though my skills are meager, Madam Yao praised my resolve to kill my enemies, saying that was just the "variable" she sought. That's why she assigned me to temporarily serve under her command for this expedition. And look at that... it's brought us back together again!

t_ch: 可巧，匹诺康尼时我也曾想过，如能和恩公多同行几日就好了——毕竟，小女子被幻胧夺去的缘分，还没全收回来呢。
t_en: What fortune! I wished I could travel with you longer back in Penacony, benefactor. After all, I've yet to reclaim the opportunities Phantylia stole from me.

t_ch: 这位爻老板人怎么样？
t_en: What kind of person is Madam Yao?

t_ch: 小女子可说不好。就像她卜的卦，如谜如雾，令人捉摸不透。也许除却从不现身的{RUBY_B#有无}「尘冥将军」{RUBY_E#}外，帝弓天将中就属这位大人最神秘莫测了。
t_en: Hard to say. Like the divinations she casts, she's a riddle shrouded in fog. Apart from the ever-absent {RUBY_B#Youwu}Patina Justice{RUBY_E#}, she's likely the most mysterious of the Arbiter Generals.

t_ch: 爻老板对你好吗？
t_en: How is Madam Yao treating you?

t_ch: 想来应该不至有性命之忧吧~恩公莫怪，跟在这位爻老板的身旁，小女子也习惯起随时开玩笑了。
t_en: I'm sure there's no mortal danger, teehee... Please forgive my jest, benefactor. Being around Madam Yao has gotten me into the habit of cracking jokes all the time.

t_ch: 这位将军虽说行事莫测，但却总能先一步予你所求，所以她差遣的人纵有腹诽，也会尽心办事。仔细想来，这位比起卜者更像是绝顶的商人？
t_en: Her actions may seem inscrutable, but she always provides what you need ahead of time. That's why even those who grumble still carry out her orders with diligence. In truth, doesn't she seem more like a shrewd merchant than a diviner?

t_ch: 唉，帝弓天将嘛，又有哪个是平庸之辈呢？
t_en: But then, when have any Arbiter Generals ever been ordinary?

t_ch: 得空来看你！
t_en: I'll drop by to see you when I get the chance!

t_ch: 恩公可不要食言喔！我们可以找一间当地酒馆，举杯闲话。
t_en: Don't go back on your word, benefactor! We can find a local bar and share a drink while we chat.

t_ch: 爻老板嘱咐，若她遁去身形，要我去约定的地方见面。时候不早了，小女子先行告退了。
t_en: Madam Yao instructed me to meet her at our agreed spot if she disappears. It's growing late, so I'll take my leave now.

t_ch: …欢愉的面具，能映照出灵魂的形状？
t_en: ...A mask of elation can reflect the shape of one's soul?

t_ch: 老日，能看看你的面具吗？
t_en: Sunny, can I take a look at your mask?

t_ch: 好啊。
t_en: Sure.

t_ch: 在匹诺康尼的装饰艺术里，乌鸦，通常会被用来表现人类梦境中潜在的渴望和低语。
t_en: In Penacony's decorative arts, ravens often symbolize the hidden desires and whispers of humans' dreamscapes.

t_ch: 你可别太投入啊。
t_en: Don't get too carried away now.

t_ch: 放心吧，我曾有过试图登神之举，不会被谒神的殊荣所迷惑。
t_en: Don't worry, I once tried to ascend to godhood myself... I won't be dazzled by the honor of an audience with a deity.

t_ch: 参与这场盛事，也是为了能更为深入地了解这片…「乐园」。就像人们身处匹诺康尼时，不能错过谐乐大典一样。
t_en: Taking part in this grand event is also about gaining a deeper understanding of this... "arcadia." Just as one cannot miss the Charmony Festival when visiting Penacony.

t_ch: 我们会成为敌人吗？
t_en: Will we become enemies?

t_ch: 唔？没有哪条规则要求幻月游戏的谒者必须彼此厮杀吧？
t_en: Hmm? There's no rule saying that Supplicants of the Phantasmoon Games have to fight each other, right?

t_ch: 恰恰相反，对「欢愉」来说，笑声不该是从彼此内心发出的共鸣吗？
t_en: On the contrary, for the Elation, shouldn't laughter be a resonance that comes from within each other's hearts?

t_ch: 这副面具到底有什么作用？
t_en: What's the deal with this mask anyway?

t_ch: 它扣在我脸上后，我似乎察觉到了一些…有趣的声音。
t_en: After putting it on, I seemed to hear... curious voices.

t_ch: 面具在向我低语：要我向神明展露自己的「欢愉」，尽力创造奇迹让乐园的民众为之欢笑。
t_en: The mask whispers to me... urging me to show my "elation" to the god, to create miracles that will make the people of the arcadia laugh with joy.

t_ch: 此刻这正是契机！
t_en: Now would be the perfect moment!

t_ch: 踏上欢愉命途的星期日！
t_en: Sunday will now embark on the Path of Elation!

t_ch: 哈哈，是个不错的笑话。
t_en: Haha, that's a good one!

t_ch: 等那位爻光将军再度现身，我该去和她谈谈合作的计划了。祝你好运，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: When that General Yao Guang shows up again, I should discuss our collaboration plans with her. Good luck, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 星际和平公司、假面愚者、仙舟将军、星核猎手、还有无名客……
t_en: The IPC, Masked Fools, a Xianzhou general, the Stellaron Hunters, and the Nameless...

t_ch: 有些为同盟而来，有些为星神的游戏而来，无论为了什么，风暴已在这个世界的天空中汇聚成形了。
t_en: Some came for the alliance, some for the Aeon's game... Whatever their reasons, a storm is brewing in the skies over this world.

t_ch: 不过眼前至少听到了个好消息，星期日加入了幻月游戏，在和火花的对垒中，他可以帮上我们的忙。
t_en: But at least we got some good news. Sunday has joined the Phantasmoon Games. He can help us in our showdown against Sparxie.

t_ch: 去「酒馆」和花火碰头吧，接下来，我们要弄清火花加入幻月游戏的目标，还有她到底想在绘世学院中策划一场怎样的「火花大会」。
t_en: Let's rendezvous with Sparkle at the tavern. We must uncover why Sparxie entered the Phantasmoon Games and what sort of "SparxiCon" she's got planned for Graphia Academy.

t_ch: 所以该怎么才能进入酒馆？
t_en: So how do we get into the tavern?

t_ch: 我小时候就听过一些都市传说，说「酒馆」的入口存在于这片土地上的每一扇门后。但不经邀请，或是没有机缘寻得路径的人，就无法踏入其中。
t_en: When I was little, I heard a few urban legends. They say the entrance to the tavern exists behind every single door in this land. But those who aren't invited, or haven't found their destined path, can never step inside.

t_ch: 这一次，我们已经手握愚者的邀请了。至于开门的钥匙…名片的背面还有一首打油诗：「若想痛饮喧闹，不必费心寻找，留神愚者谜题之邀，引你寻得门扉之钥。」
t_en: This time, we already have the Fools' invitation in hand. As for the key to open the door... there's a silly rhyme on the back of the business card: "For revelry and drinks galore, no need to search from door to door. Heed the Fool's riddle with care, to find the key that leads you there."

t_ch: 谜语人又来了？
t_en: Ugh, the Riddlers again?

t_ch: 这押韵还不如乱破。
t_en: That rhyme's worse than what Rappa comes up with.

t_ch: 所以，{NICKNAME}，关于「愚者的谜题之邀」…你有什么头绪吗？
t_en: So, {NICKNAME}, about the Fool's Riddle... got any leads?

t_ch: 许久之前——你暗自吐槽，都过去这么久了，谁还记得？但你确实记住了，在花火第一次见到你时，她曾对你说起过一个谜语。
t_en: A long time ago... You scoff to yourself, who even remembers after all this time? But you do remember. When Sparkle first met you, she told you a riddle.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>无论抬头或是低头，人类凝望天空时，看到的都是同一轮幻月。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Whether they look up or down, the moon that humanity gazes upon in the sky is always the same Phantasmoon.</color>

t_ch: 抬头还能理解，既然低下了头，又要怎么凝望天空呢？
t_en: Looking up makes sense. But looking down... how exactly do you gaze at the sky?

t_ch: 看着水中的天空。
t_en: By watching the sky reflected in water.

t_ch: 看着倒影的天空。
t_en: By gazing at the reflected sky.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，看来你和「酒馆」确实有缘。我明白了，谜底是鸽川。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, it seems fate ties you to the tavern. I get it now. The answer is Dovebrook.

t_ch: 这趟度假之旅，似乎又回到了咱们最熟悉的「麻烦重重」的状态。
t_en: Looks like this vacation trip has gone right back to our usual "trouble magnet" mode.

t_ch: 这才是无名客不得不品的假期。
t_en: Now this is the kind of vacation the Nameless just has to savor.

t_ch: 刚才就在这条街，我被一只狸猫偷拍了…
t_en: A furbo snapped a pic of me on this street without my consent just now...

t_ch: 无名客有了名气，这可不是什么好事。
t_en: Fame isn't exactly good news for the Nameless.

t_ch: 鸽川区，前面就到了。
t_en: We're almost at Dovebrook District.

t_ch: 狸了个大谱！浣熊大人，你真的来啦！
t_en: Holy furboly! Great Raccoon, you really came!

t_ch: 这儿的事情办得差不多了。算算时间，丹恒与星期日那边也快到了。方便起见，我会通知他们来杂志社碰头。
t_en: We're pretty much done here, and Dan Heng and Sunday should be arriving soon as well. I'll just have them meet us here at the publishing house.

t_ch: 唔，这里变化还挺大的……？
t_en: Hmm, this place seems to have changed a lot...

t_ch: 有只狸猫在喊你，过去瞧瞧吧？
t_en: There's a furbo calling out to you. Why don't we go take a look?

t_ch: 社长大人，请您在电脑前坐好哩！我来告诉您怎么操作~
t_en: Please take a seat in front of the computer, President! I'll show you how it works~

t_ch: …天灵灵地灵灵…
t_en: ...Spirit of the Sky, Spirit of the Earth...

t_ch: …求求阿哈显显灵！
t_en: ...Please, Aha, show me a sign!

t_ch: 赐我们一个新社长呶~
t_en: Please give us a new company president~

t_ch: 也许会像整个绘世家族那样，只剩下空有虚名的外壳。
t_en: Perhaps they've become like the Graphia family: nothing but empty shells of their former glory.

t_ch: …这里就是「世界尽头」酒馆吗？
t_en: ...So this is the "World's End" Tavern?

t_ch: 不不不，我必须纠正你。你要是以为这儿是「酒馆」的全部，那可就大错特错了。
t_en: No, no, no, allow me to correct you. If you think this is all there is to the tavern, you're sorely mistaken.

t_ch: 就像一大串葡萄上的一颗果实。这儿只是「酒馆」与这个世界相接的一部分……
t_en: Like a single grape on a great bunch, this is just the part where the tavern connects to this world...

t_ch: 抱歉，但这儿看起来…似乎只是一间普通的酒吧？
t_en: Sorry, but this looks... like a regular lounge?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，所谓的「二相乐园」也不是什么「乐园」，有人有为此抱怨什么吗？生活不还是照样过嘛？
t_en: Hahaha, the so-called "Planarcadia" isn't really an "arcadia" either, but who's complaining? Life goes on, doesn't it?

t_ch: 如果你要找「名副其实」的东西，应该登上星穹列车看看嘛！你知道吗，它居然真的是一架列车，上面找不到任何宇航动力学特征！
t_en: If you're looking for something that's "true to its name," take a ride on the Astral Express! Funny thing is, it's literally a train, you know? Can't find a single trace of astronautical engineering on it!

t_ch: 呃…等等，你俩是…星穹列车的领航员，还有车上的浣熊，对吧？我在新闻里见过你们。
t_en: Uh... wait, you two are... Navigator of the Astral Express, and the raccoon on board, right? I've seen you in the news.

t_ch: 初次见面，多多指教。
t_en: Nice to meet you.

t_ch: 什么浣熊？是银河球棒侠！
t_en: What raccoon? I'm the Galactic Baseballer!

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈哈，朋友们，来大人物啦！来大笑话啦！
t_en: HAHAHA! Friends, a big shot is here! A real knee-slapper incoming!

t_ch: 真是列车的人？不是什么角色扮演爱好者吧？——
t_en: Are they really from the Express? You're not some cosplay enthusiast, are you?

t_ch: 列车的人也来酒馆？他们有幽默感吗？——
t_en: Express folks come to the tavern too? Do they even have a sense of humor?

t_ch: 嗨嗨嗨，给我签个名好吧？就签脚底上，我这辈子不洗——
t_en: Hey hey hey, can I get your autograph? Sign the sole of my foot! I'll never wash it again!

t_ch: 兄弟姐妹们，收收好奇心！这两位是我请来的客人！就算是最调皮的孩子也要适可而止，保持礼貌哟！
t_en: Everyone, curb your curiosity! These two are my guests. Even the most mischievous among you should know when to stop, and mind your manners!

t_ch: 哟哟，这不是小花火吗？你的笑话我们大家可都听说了……
t_en: Well, well, if it isn't little Sparkle? We've all heard your joke...

t_ch: 「假面愚者」去掉假面，那不就只剩下一个蠢货了吗——
t_en: Take the Mask from a Masked Fool, and what's left's but a fool...

t_ch: 哎呀呀，「假面愚者」没了蠢货，只有一张空空如也的面具岂不是更好？
t_en: Oh my, wouldn't a Masked Fool be better off without the fool? An empty mask sounds even better, no?

t_ch: 你把他怎么了？
t_en: What did you do to him?

t_ch: 让他保持两小时的沉默。如果没人把它丢进「黄金告解室」冲掉的话，两小时后，他还能再来找我一次麻烦。
t_en: Put him on mute for two hours. If no one flushes him down the "Golden Confessional," he'll be back to hassle me again in two hours.

t_ch: 他还活着吗？
t_en: Is he still alive?

t_ch: 算是…活着吧？要看你怎么定义「活着」。大部分愚者甚至都不肯承认自己活着，所以他也不能完全算是死了。
t_en: Alive... ish? Depends how you define "alive." Most Fools won't even admit they're alive, so he isn't exactly dead either.

t_ch: 他刚才说「你的笑话」…？
t_en: Did he just say "your joke"...?

t_ch: 别太把傻子的发言当回事，除非你想和他排排坐？
t_en: Don't take an idiot's words too seriously, unless you want to join them?

t_ch: 好了，这下终于可以不被打扰地接待客人啦。两位，来这边坐坐，我们聊聊关于那个冒牌货的事情。
t_en: Alright, now I can finally entertain my guests without interruptions. You two, this way. Let's talk about the impostor.

t_ch: 喂喂，小灰毛，你要去哪儿？
t_en: Hey hey, Gray Hair, where are you headed?

t_ch: 男人疲倦的目光上下打量眼前的年轻人片刻。他微微颔首致意。
t_en: The man's weary gaze sizes up the young person before him for a moment. He offers a slight nod in greeting.

t_ch: 你好奇的目光在他的身旁逡巡，他带着一只会说话的…猴？
t_en: Your curious gaze circles him. Is that thing by his side a... talking monkey?

t_ch: 睡蕉小猴？
t_en: A Slumbernana Monkey?

t_ch: 那是什么？
t_en: What's that?

t_ch: 你的猴子会说话？
t_en: Your monkey can talk?

t_ch: 他不是我的。他的自由属于他自己。
t_en: He's not mine. His freedom belongs to himself.

t_ch: 小朋友，我想你一定把我和你之前见过的某些猿科生物混为一谈了吧？
t_en: Kid, I believe you're confusing me with some other primates you've met.

t_ch: 抱歉，看走眼了。
t_en: Sorry, my mistake.

t_ch: 来吧两位，为了迎接你们，进酒吧的第一杯我请，喝点什么？
t_en: Alright, you two, first round's on me. What'll it be?

t_ch: 未成年人，来杯水。
t_en: I'm underage. A glass of water please.

t_ch: 给我最烈！最劲！的饮料！
t_en: Give me the strongest and most hardcore drink you've got!

t_ch: 不用了，我想保持清醒。
t_en: No thanks. I'd like to stay sober.

t_ch: 哈，这儿是「酒馆」。就算一滴不沾，愚者们的笑话与恶作剧也一定会让你上下颠倒，忘记天地为何物。
t_en: Ha! This is a tavern. Even if you don't drink a drop, the Fools' jokes and pranks will turn your world upside down until you can't tell sky from ground!

t_ch: 直接说正事吧！
t_en: Let's cut to the chase!

t_ch: 我们不是来喝酒的，时间分秒流逝。关于火花，你查到什么了吗？
t_en: We're not here to drink. Time's ticking. Did you find anything on Sparxie?

t_ch: 别板着个脸嘛，会让桶里的佳酿变酸的。你们没到的时候，勤劳的小花火一直在搜集情报，连一分钟都没闲着。
t_en: Don't pull a long face. You'll sour the vintage in the cask. While you were gone, diligent little Sparkle was gathering intel non-stop, and didn't waste a single minute.

t_ch: 火花…她确实假扮成我的模样来过这儿。
t_en: Sparxie... She did come here disguised as me.

t_ch: 我打了个电话给乔瓦尼，也许你们不记得他了…他是最近抽签负责管理寄存区的倒霉蛋。老乔告诉我，一周前，「我」来这儿取走了几样东西……
t_en: I called Giovanni. You might not remember him... he's the poor sap who drew the short straw to manage the storage area recently. Old Gio told me that a week ago, "I" came here and took a few things...

t_ch: 其中有一罐模因病毒。
t_en: Among them was a vial of memetic virus.

t_ch: 模因病毒…也难怪她能控制绘世学院，扭曲三月和瓦尔特的认知，让他们误以为自己是学生和老师。
t_en: A memetic virus... No wonder she could take over Graphia Academy and distort March and Welt's cognition, making them believe they're a student and teacher.

t_ch: 她要把所有人变成猴子？
t_en: She wants to turn everyone into monkeys?

t_ch: 她要把所有人变成傻子？
t_en: She wants to turn everyone into fools?

t_ch: 她要和原始博士抢生意？
t_en: She wants to undercut Dr. Primitive's business?

t_ch: 八九不离十了，<color=#dbc291ff>所谓的「火花大会」就是散布模因病毒的幌子。</color>
t_en: Close enough. The <color=#dbc291ff>so-called "SparxiCon" is a front for spreading a memetic virus</color>.

t_ch: 光知道这一点信息还不够，她要散布什么类型的模因？发病的特征是什么？对她的计划和行动了解更多，才有破解之法。
t_en: Knowing that isn't enough. What type of memetic virus is she spreading? What are the symptoms? We need more on her plans and actions before we can counter it.

t_ch: 这我也想到啦，所以我拜访了几位朋友，问出了这个冒牌货出没的地点。要查出更多详细的信息，我们得亲自动手。
t_en: Thought of that already. So I visited a few friends and learned where the imposter's been haunting. To dig up details, we'll have to get hands-on.

t_ch: 带路吧。
t_en: Lead the way then.

t_ch: 入此门者，当舍弃一切绝望。想过去，就得取悦我。
t_en: Abandon all despair, ye who enter. If you want to pass, then amuse me.

t_ch: 对了，受累问一句，你…不是阿哈本尊吧？
t_en: By the way, hate to ask... You're not Aha THEMSELF, are you?

t_ch: 不是！
t_en: No!

t_ch: 那真是太好了。呵呵，反正就算你是，我也没法辨别你、拦住你，对不？
t_en: Well, that's a relief. Heh, not like I could tell if you were or stop you, right?

t_ch: 没错，我就是阿哈！
t_en: That's right, I am Aha!

t_ch: 不，你不是阿哈。呵呵，虽然你有股子会来事儿的气息，但你不是。
t_en: No, you're not Aha. Heh. You've got that smooth-talker air, but you're not.

t_ch: 如果你是阿哈的话，你挥挥手就能让我不再忧郁。你做不到吧？
t_en: If you were Aha, a wave of your hand would cure my melancholy. You can't do that, can you?

t_ch: 是不是有什么关系啊？
t_en: What does that have to do with anything?

t_ch: 这儿不是「欢愉」的酒馆吗？难道你们不欢迎乐子神本尊降临？
t_en: Isn't this the tavern of the Elation? Don't you welcome The Laughter gracing you with THEIR presence?

t_ch: 你错了，并非不受欢迎——看到了吗？愚者们在那边立了块牌子：「阿哈、悲悼伶人禁止进入！」…是禁止进入！
t_en: You're wrong. It's not that THEY are unwelcome. See that? The Fools put up a sign over there: "Aha and Mourning Actors stay clear from the door!" ...Stay clear!

t_ch: 虽然听起来很可笑，但…这是真的。在六百六十六次邀请阿哈没得到回音后，年轻一代的愚者们集体投票把乐子神开除出了「欢愉」命途。
t_en: Ridiculous as it sounds... it's true. After six hundred and sixty-six invitations to Aha went unanswered, the younger Fools voted to expel The Laughter from the Path of Elation.

t_ch: 我懂，就像学生们把老师开除出教室，毫无意义，但气势很足。
t_en: I get it. It's like students expelling a teacher from a classroom. The action is meaningless... The message is loud and clear, though.

t_ch: 好了，大门先生，我向你保证，咱们三个里面没有阿哈，也没有悲悼伶人。
t_en: All right, Mr. Doorway, I assure you that none of us three is Aha, and none is a Mourning Actor.

t_ch: 唷，是花火啊。哼，是花火吗？不是其他人假扮的花火吧？算了，我分不清，也不在乎。
t_en: Ho, it's Sparkle. Hmph, is it really Sparkle? It's not someone else pretending to be Sparkle? I can't tell, and I don't care.

t_ch: 听好了，灰头发的{F#姑娘}{M#小伙}，想让我打开，就得给我来个笑话当是过路费，我一直在等那个能治好我忧郁的笑话。
t_en: Listen up, gray-haired {M#lad.}{F#lass.} If you want me to open, pay the toll with saying a joke. I've been waiting for the one joke that can cure my melancholy.

t_ch: 现在，取悦我吧。
t_en: Now, amuse me.

t_ch: 你深深叹了口气，看来你得露一手了——
t_en: You let out a deep sigh. Looks like you'll have to show them what you're made of—

t_ch: 「笑话」。
t_en: "A joke."

t_ch: 呃？然后呢…？
t_en: Uh? And then...?

t_ch: 我说了，「笑话」。
t_en: I said it. "A joke."

t_ch: 门沉默了片刻。
t_en: The door fell silent for a moment.

t_ch: 可怜的孩子…比起忧郁来说，还是讲一个烂笑话更可怜一些。
t_en: Poor child... A bad joke is more pitiful than melancholy.

t_ch: 想听笑话？没、门！
t_en: Want a joke? No. Way!

t_ch: 呃？什么？
t_en: Huh? What?

t_ch: 你没听懂吗？「没有笑话，门先生」和「我希望没有门」，你想怎么理解都行。阿基维利在上，我竟然沦落到了主动给一扇门解释笑话的地步……
t_en: Didn't catch it? "No joke, Mr. Doorway," or "There's no way I'll tell a joke." Interpret as you like. By Akivili above, I can't believe I've come to explaining jokes to a door...

t_ch: 门沉默了片刻。被你的烂笑话击中，它突然发出一阵骇人的大笑，像是被一连串机枪子弹打中，陷入死一般的沉默。
t_en: The door fell silent for a moment. Struck by your terrible joke, it suddenly burst into a terrifying laugh, like being hit by a barrage of machine gun fire, before falling into a deathly silence.

t_ch: 打开门？「打」开门？
t_en: Be a-door-able and open the door now!

t_ch: 不过是一扇门罢了，什么破笑话收集器。也许门后面根本只是躲着某个灵感枯竭的脱口秀演员，不断从过门而入的客人口中窃取灵感呢？
t_en: It's just a door, what a lame joke collector. Maybe there's just some uninspired stand-up comedian hiding behind it, stealing inspiration from everyone who walks through?

t_ch: 门不愿打开？你当然可以选择把它「打」开。
t_en: Door won't open? Well, that's not a-door-able.

t_ch: 我没想到，你竟然神通广大到能把我带进「酒馆」，不愧是名侦探的助手。
t_en: Didn't expect you to have the pull to get me into the tavern. As expected of a detective's assistant.

t_ch: 早些年我还有幽默感的时代，有个愚者给了我一张邀请函，感谢他吧。
t_en: Back when I still had a sense of humor, a Fool gave me an invitation. You can thank him for that.

t_ch: 你还真打算把星核猎手的案子办到底吗？那家伙能付你的可只有黑钱喔？
t_en: You're really going to see the Stellaron Hunter case through? They can only pay you in dirty money, you know.

t_ch: 不然咧？弟兄们需要钱。信用点哪分什么黑与白，只分零和一。
t_en: What else can I do? The boys need the money. Credits aren't black or white. They're ones and zeros.

t_ch: 好吧。我联系了恶水，他说骗走面具的三个人，其中有一个他似乎在新闻上见过。
t_en: All right. I reached out to Caustide. He said that one of the three people who stole the mask looked familiar. He might've seen them in the news.

t_ch: 我按他的描述画了半天疑犯肖像，怀疑那人可能是星穹列车上的无名客，丹恒。
t_en: Based on his description, I spent ages sketching the suspect's portrait, and I suspect it might be Dan Heng, the Nameless from the Astral Express.

t_ch: 恶水啊…咱们又欠了他一个人情，这下还不清啦！说起来，丹恒…是仙舟人的名字吧？我是在哪儿听过吗…耳熟得很。
t_en: Ah, Caustide... We owe him another favor. No paying this one back, huh? Speaking of which, Dan Heng... That's a Xianzhou name, isn't it? Where have I heard that name before...? It sounds so familiar.

t_ch: 所以，这三人里面哪个是丹恒？灰头发的？还是红头发的？还是那个看起来随时会作妖的黑发小姑娘？
t_en: So, which one of these three is Dan Heng? The one with gray hair? The redhead? Or that black-haired girl who looks like she's ready to stir up trouble at any moment?

t_ch: 不，你先别说答案，让我猜猜。
t_en: No, don't tell me yet. Let me guess.

t_ch: 我略懂仙舟的语言——「丹」字有一个意思是「红色」。所以丹恒…是红头发的那位小姐，对吧？
t_en: I know a bit of the Xianzhou language. The word "dan" can mean "red." So Dan Heng... is the lady with red hair, right?

t_ch: 你的推理能力还是这么感人。行吧，我直接公布答案，这三个人里哪一个都不是丹恒。你平时到底看不看新闻啊？
t_en: Your deduction skills never fail to impress. Fine, I'll just give you the answer. None of these three is Dan Heng. Do you even watch the news?

t_ch: 电视、报纸、网络上都是假消息，你让我看什么？
t_en: TV, newspapers, the internet... it's all fake news. What do you expect me to look at?

t_ch: 还是谈正事吧。我没找到丹恒行动的踪迹，像是被人抹掉了一样。
t_en: Let's get down to business. I couldn't find any trace of Dan Heng's actions. It's like someone wiped them clean.

t_ch: 所以我干脆反其道而行之，盯上了列车组里的其他人，也许顺着{F#她们}{M#他们}能找到面具的线索。
t_en: So I decided to try a different approach. Keep tabs on the other members of the Express Crew. Maybe following their trail will lead to clues about the mask.

t_ch: 干得好，老白。对方要往酒馆深处走咯，跟上我。
t_en: Nice work, Mister N. They're heading deeper into the tavern. Follow me.

t_ch: 这里是……？
t_en: Where's this...?

t_ch: 锵锵~欢迎来到「极乐之星」的炫光照耀下——
t_en: Ta-da! Welcome to the dazzling lights of the "Blisstar"!

t_ch: 酒馆的中心，二相乐园欢乐的舞池，有些没品位的假面愚者，会把这里当作保持欢笑的诀窍——
t_en: The heart of the tavern, Planarcadia's dazzling dance floor. There are some tasteless Masked Fools who consider this place the secret to keeping the laughter going...

t_ch: 假如生活残酷，别去讨公平，摇起来！把自己体内的悲伤摇匀就行了。
t_en: If life's cruel, don't beg for fairness. Dance! Shake away that sadness until it's all well-mixed with the joy in your body.

t_ch: 极乐之星？
t_en: Blisstar?

t_ch: 就是悬挂在那儿的巨大灯球啦！对酒馆来说，这东西就像天空中的幻月一样重要。有机会的话，我可以带你到里面开开眼。
t_en: That huge light orb up there! For a tavern, it's as essential as the Phantasmoon in the sky. If we get the chance, I can take you inside for a real eye-opener.

t_ch: 说起来，两个月前有个傻瓜在猛灌了几百杯以后突发奇想，打算用超霸王陀螺把它打下来。
t_en: Speaking of which, two months ago some idiot, after downing hundreds of drinks, had the bright idea to knock it down with a Super Overlord Spinning Top.

t_ch: 于是大家只好在「极乐之星」的周围加固了一圈钢架，把它密封在「画中世界」里。
t_en: So we had to reinforce the Blisstar with a steel frame around it and seal it inside the World in Canvas.

t_ch: 这么胡闹，酒保不管吗？
t_en: Doesn't the drinksmith care about this nonsense?

t_ch: 你懂的，假面愚者嘛，总爱往酒杯里兑辣椒水，冲马桶里丢炮仗。
t_en: You know how the Masked Fools are... Spiking drinks with hot sauce and tossing firecrackers down the toilet.

t_ch: 酒保是件苦差事，没人自愿担纲。打从{RUBY_B#前任酒保}布拉琪{RUBY_E#}消失后，愚者们抽签轮值，挨个担任临时的酒保。
t_en: Being a drinksmith is a tough gig. No one volunteers. Since {RUBY_B#previous drinksmith}Bragi{RUBY_E#} vanished, the Fools have been drawing lots to take turns as temporary drinksmiths.

t_ch: 阿哈不管管这儿吗？
t_en: Can't Aha take care of this place?

t_ch: …阿哈？管管？祂不都被酒馆拒之门外了吗？
t_en: ...Aha? Take care? Weren't THEY banned from the tavern?

t_ch: 总之，以那位乐子神的性格，祂要是想出手「管管」，只会怂恿某人把这儿炸个底朝天。
t_en: And given The Laughter's personality, if THEY "took care" of it, THEY'd just egg someone on to blow the place sky-high.

t_ch: 你说的这个愚者，不会是火花吧？
t_en: This Fool you're talking about... It wouldn't happen to be Sparxie, would it?

t_ch: 如果是的话，我现在就该被当成替罪羊，绑在灯球上了。
t_en: If it were, I'd already be tied to that light orb as the scapegoat.

t_ch: 言归正传，接下来咱们要去「黄金告解室」，目击证人说火花去过那儿。
t_en: Anyway, the next stop is the Golden Confessional. The eyewitness says Sparxie was there.

t_ch: 「黄金告解室」…是什么地方？
t_en: The Golden Confessional... What kind of place is that?

t_ch: 是个让人放下一切烦恼的地方。哎，说再多也不如亲眼看看，走吧。
t_en: A place where you can let go of all your worries. Well, words won't do it justice. Let's go see it for yourself.

t_ch: 目击者说，一周前「我」来过酒馆，灌下几杯「炸弹的第一滴」后，来到了这儿。
t_en: Witnesses say a week ago "I" came to the bar, knocked back a few shots of "Bomb's First Drop," then headed here.

t_ch: 「黄金告解室」，豪饮过量的愚者们的救急所。在这儿，咱们抛下多余的部分，轻装上阵前往下一秒。
t_en: The Golden Confessional is an emergency shelter for Fools who've had one drink too many. Here, we shed our excess baggage before marching light-footed into the next second.

t_ch: 这不就是…厕所？
t_en: Wait... isn't this just a bathroom?

t_ch: 黄金告解室就是黄金告解室，它可不是什么厕所！
t_en: The Golden Confessional is THE Golden Confessional, not some random bathroom!

t_ch: 黄金…王座？
t_en: A golden... throne?

t_ch: 这下我老桑博可成了无冕之王喽~
t_en: Well well, looks like ol' Sampo's become the king without a crown!

t_ch: 还好，没有想象中的味道…
t_en: Phew, it doesn't smell as bad as I imagined...

t_ch: 当然了！这儿只是大家释放坏情绪的地方而已。
t_en: Of course! This is just a place where everyone comes to let off some steam.

t_ch: 又见面了，老朋友。听花火说你要来酒馆，老桑博这心里啊，真是装满了高兴！
t_en: Well, well, if it isn't my old friend! Sparkle said you'd be coming to the tavern. Old Sampo's heart is bursting with joy!

t_ch: 当然，还有一点点哀伤。你说邀请我天字第一号{F#好姐妹}{M#好哥们}进「酒馆」喝一杯的主意，怎么就让她花火占了先？
t_en: And a teensy bit of sorrow. How did Sparkle beat me to inviting my number-one {M#buddy}{F#bestie} to the tavern for a drink?

t_ch: 桑博，是你啊！
t_en: Sampo, it's you!

t_ch: 想我了吗，老朋友！
t_en: Missed me, old friend?

t_ch: 我就知道在这儿准能见到你！
t_en: I knew I'd find you here!

t_ch: 「就知道」？你怎么就把老桑博和这儿联系在了一起？这话有点伤人了，{F#姐们}{M#哥们}。
t_en: You knew? And what made you link me to this place? That's kinda harsh, {M#bro.}{F#sis.}

t_ch: 你好啊，姬子小姐，好久不见啦。
t_en: Hello, Miss Himeko. Long time no see.

t_ch: 桑博先生，你好啊。这一回你为什么生意而来？
t_en: Mr. Sampo, hello. What business brings you here this time?

t_ch: 跟生意无关，只是来偿还一点人情旧债罢了。
t_en: No business, just here to repay an old favor.

t_ch: 我们面前这位愚者小姐向我发来一条求助短信。说有人冒充她的模样在「酒馆」招摇过市。
t_en: Miss Fool here sent me a message asking for h-e-l-p. Someone's been parading around the tavern, impersonating her.

t_ch: 这可真是条大新闻！从来只有花火大人冒充别人，没想到有一天玩鹰的也会被鹰啄了眼——
t_en: Well, now this is some juicy news! Miss Sparkle's usually the one impersonating others, but look how the tables have turned. The trickster's getting a taste of her own medicine!

t_ch: 闭嘴吧，桑博。我让你来是来偿还人情的，可没让你来传我的八卦。
t_en: Shush, Sampo. I brought you here to repay a favor, not to gossip about me.

t_ch: 嗨呀，我也经历过丢失面具的窘境，多亏花火大人大发慈悲，老桑博才不至于走投无路。这不，今天您有求，我必应！
t_en: Oh, I've suffered the embarrassment of losing my mask myself. Thanks to Miss Sparkle's mercy, I didn't end up stranded. So here I am today, ready to help you out, no questions asked!

t_ch: 那就好，咱们开工吧。
t_en: Alright then, let's get to work!

t_ch: 开工？要做什么？
t_en: Get to work? What are we doing?

t_ch: 当然是…从这些人类倾吐烦恼的「宝座」中，翻找那位火花小姐留在此处的烦恼。
t_en: Naturally... We'll fish around in these "thrones" where humans dump their worries and find the worries that Miss Sparxie left behind.

t_ch: 你想让我们帮你…通马桶？
t_en: You want us to... unclog your toilet?

t_ch: 在这儿？翻找烦恼？
t_en: Here? Dig up worries?

t_ch: 不要开这种玩笑！
t_en: Don't make this kind of jokes!

t_ch: 我可没开玩笑。
t_en: I'm not joking.

t_ch: 两位也别太惊讶，先让我来科普一下假面愚者保持心态健康的小秘密——
t_en: Don't be too shocked, you two. Let me share a little secret about how we Masked Fools stay mentally healthy...

t_ch: 喝下「炸弹的第一滴」之类的解忧饮品，身体里的有害忆质：什么痛苦啊、烦恼就会一股脑儿地被催吐出来。
t_en: Drink a mood-lifter like "Bomb's First Drop," and all the harmful memoria in your body — you know, the pain, the worries — gets purged in one go.

t_ch: 愚者们就是靠这个乐乐呵呵地，毫无心理负担地进行各种恶作剧了。
t_en: That's how Fools pull pranks and have a blast without a shred of guilt.

t_ch: 所以，这个厕所的地下沉积着愚者们的忆质。而你想从里面找出火花的烦恼，捕捉她行动前的想法？
t_en: So all that memoria has settled under this bathroom. And you want to sift through it to find Sparxie's worries... and catch her thoughts before she acts?

t_ch: 运气好的话，也许还能挖出如何打败她的秘密。怎么了，大姐头，你脸色不太对劲？
t_en: If luck's on our side, maybe we'll even dig up the secret to beating her. What's wrong, ma'am? You don't look so hot.

t_ch: 对要做的事，我没意见，但是…徒手干这个？我拒绝。
t_en: I've got no objection to the task, but... doing this bare-handed? I refuse.

t_ch: 放心吧，老桑博办事，哪次不是有备而来的？放心，绝不会让你捡个大便宜！
t_en: Don't worry, when has ol' Sampo ever come unprepared? Trust me, I won't let you get off that easy!

t_ch: 「情绪冲击钻」！
t_en: Emotional Impact Drill!

t_ch: 对不起，拿错了，是这玩意儿。
t_en: Sorry, wrong gadget. This one.

t_ch: 你们听说过寻水棒吗？…算了，原理我就不啰嗦说明了。
t_en: Hey, have you heard of a dowsing rod? Well ...Forget it, I'll spare you the theory.

t_ch: 我把这玩意儿的钻头和花火的脾气做了同调，它会钻出类似情绪的忆质喷泉。
t_en: I tuned the drill bit to resonate with Sparkle's temperament. It'll drill out memoria fountains with similar emotions.

t_ch: 不过丑话说在前头，用的时候可得注意安全啊！毕竟火花来这儿都是一周前的事情了，要是钻出些其他什么怪东西，你们可得提防着点。
t_en: But fair warning: Use it carefully! It's been a week since Sparxie was here. Keep your guard up in case something else comes bursting out.

t_ch: 这么多马桶，从哪儿找起？
t_en: With so many toilets, where do we start?

t_ch: 要问老桑博的话，答案只能是碰碰运气了。
t_en: Well, If you ask me... leave it to luck.

t_ch: 我们哪儿来的时间碰运气？我的雷达告诉我，那个自高自大的冒牌货要挑地方排出烦恼，也一定会挑最高的——
t_en: We don't have time for luck. My radar says that pompous imposter would pick the highest spot to vent...

t_ch: 没错，就是那个「黄金宝座」。去试试看吧！
t_en: Exactly, the "Golden Throne." Give it a try!

t_ch: {F#姐们}{M#哥们}，你自己选个下钻的地方，开始掘金吧！
t_en: {M#Bro,}{F#Girl,} pick a spot to drill and start mining that gold!

t_ch: 桶里传来激烈的水声，仿佛人类的烦恼想要喷薄涌出。
t_en: Violent splashing sounds came from the bucket, as if human worries were about to burst forth.

t_ch: 突然，有什么东西从下水道里出现了！
t_en: Suddenly, something emerged from the sewer!

t_ch: 火花的烦恼出来吧！
t_en: Come forth, Sparxie's worries!

t_ch: 这是什么玩意啊？
t_en: What in the world is this?

t_ch: 啊？这看起来可不像是她的烦恼。
t_en: Huh? That doesn't seem like something she'd be worried about.

t_ch: 枉我费尽心机，小心翼翼地混进来。我还以为被发现就会壮烈成仁呢……
t_en: After going through all this trouble to sneak in... Thought I'd go down in a blaze of glory if I got caught...

t_ch: …这个地方到底是怎么回事？！假面愚者堕落成这个模样了吗？
t_en: ...What the heck is going on with this place!? Have the Masked Fools really fallen this far?

t_ch: 你是…？
t_en: You are...?

t_ch: 我啊，是一个悲悼伶人！很明显的吧。
t_en: Me? I'm a Mourning Actor! Isn't it obvious?

t_ch: 完全看不出来。
t_en: Not in the least.

t_ch: 你到底哪儿悲伤了？
t_en: What exactly makes you "mourning"?

t_ch: 你看不出来吗？我现在就很悲伤啊！
t_en: Can't you tell? I'm very sad right now!

t_ch: 不妨告诉你吧，我身负着一项悲壮的使命：「酒馆」下达了禁止伶人入内的禁令后，我苦心孤诣潜入此处，并向他们揭示「命运恒久悲哀」的真理。
t_en: Let me tell you about my tragic mission: After the tavern issued a ban on Mourning Actors, I painstakingly snuck in here to reveal to them the truth that "destiny is eternally sorrowful."

t_ch: 你成功了啊！
t_en: You pulled it off!?

t_ch: 我失败了！我粉碎了潜入过程中的每一个障碍——但潜入的结果却粉碎了我！
t_en: I failed! I smashed every obstacle during the infiltration, only for the outcome to smash me!

t_ch: 昨天，我在吧台向大家揭示我身份的谜底，并且向他们讲述了一个悲壮的故事，做好了准备接受他们最残酷的刑罚和最遥远的流放。
t_en: Yesterday, I went on stage at the bar and revealed my true identity to everyone and told them a tragic tale, bracing myself for their harshest punishment and farthest exile.

t_ch: 可是他们…这帮愚者在台下不停吹口哨，还有人给我买了好几轮饮料。有人甚至走上台拥抱我，夸我是「史无前例的假面愚者」。
t_en: But they... these Fools kept whistling from below, and some bought me several rounds. Someone even came up on stage to hug me, praising me as "the unprecedented Masked Fool".

t_ch: 这真是…太羞辱人了！
t_en: This is... utterly humiliating!

t_ch: 桶里传来激烈的水声，仿佛人类的烦恼想要喷薄涌出。
t_en: Violent splashing sounds came from the bucket, as if human worries were about to burst forth.

t_ch: 突然，有什么东西从下水道里出现了！
t_en: Suddenly, something emerged from the sewer!

t_ch: 这是火花的烦恼吗？
t_en: Is this what's bothering Sparxie?

t_ch: 这是什么玩意啊？
t_en: What in the world is this?

t_ch: 当心啊{F#姐们}{M#哥们}！这是哪个愚者胡乱塞进下水道的怪物吧？
t_en: Watch out, {M#bro!}{F#sis!} Must be some weird thing a Fool dumped into the sewers, huh?

t_ch: 你靠近这散发无上威光的宝座，毫无疑问，它是纯金的。
t_en: You draw near the throne that is radiating an aura of supreme power. It's unquestionably made of solid gold.

t_ch: 构成它的每一颗原子曾来自中子星的碰撞、超新星的爆发……但假面愚者却将星星们珍贵的泪水锻造成了承接人类烦恼的器皿。也许在他们眼中，这样便幽了宇宙一默。
t_en: Every atom that makes it up came from neutron star collisions and supernova bursts... Yet the Masked Fools forged these precious tears of the stars into vessels to hold human worries. Perhaps in their eyes, this was their way of playing a cosmic joke.

t_ch: 你发现一个问题，比起这王座的高大，你的身材委实渺小，没法将钻头探进水里。
t_en: You notice a problem: Compared to the towering throne, you're way too small to reach and dip the Drill Bit into the water.

t_ch: 桑博，你刚才是怎么上去的？
t_en: Sampo, how did you get up there just now?

t_ch: 瞧我这记性，走边上那条管道。
t_en: Oh right, silly me. Through that pipe on the side.

t_ch: 黄金宝座散发着令人目眩的威严光晕，你真怕自己脚一滑跌入其中。
t_en: The golden throne emanates a dazzling aura of authority. You're genuinely afraid you might slip and fall right into it.

t_ch: 你猛力将钻头探入马桶！
t_en: You forcefully thrust the drill bit into the toilet!

t_ch: 突然，马桶开始急促向外喷水，有什么东西从下水道里出现了！
t_en: Suddenly, the toilet starts gushing water violently, and something emerges from the drain!

t_ch: 火花的烦恼，出来！
t_en: Come out, Sparxie's worries!

t_ch: 看，我说什么来着，那个冒牌货的烦恼出现了！
t_en: See? What did I tell you? The impostor's worries have appeared!

t_ch: 真是件稀罕事！过去火花花一直都是顺着网络和大家亲切交流，生平第一次居然顺着下水道网络和自己的粉丝见面了。
t_en: What a rare sight! Sparxicle used to always connect with everyone online. But now, for the first time, she's meeting her fans through the sewer network!

t_ch: 抱歉，我们可是你的黑粉！
t_en: Sorry, but we're your haters!

t_ch: 老实交代吧，你偷走那筒模因病毒想用它做什么？是想借着「火花大会」的幌子把大家变成唯命是从的白痴，还是上蹿下跳的猴子？
t_en: Come clean now! What are you planning to do with that memetic virus you stole? Use SparxiCon as a front to turn everyone into mindless zombies, or dancing monkeys?

t_ch: 我怎么会伤害我最最亲爱的粉丝们呢？
t_en: How could I ever harm my dearest, most beloved fans?

t_ch: 你劫持了一整座学校的师生，把他们变成你的傀儡，为你加入「幻月游戏」鞍前马后效劳——你该不会想狡辩自己是为他们做了这些的吧？
t_en: You took an entire school of teachers and students hostage and turned them into your puppets to help you enter the Phantasmoon Games. You're going to argue that you did it all "for them"?

t_ch: 那还用问！打从我被创造出来的第一天起，我就一直在执行我的创造者要求我做的事情——倾听每个粉丝的提问，用心揣摩他们的痛苦和烦恼。
t_en: Of course I did! From the very first day I was created, I've been doing exactly what my creator asked of me: listening to every fan's questions, empathizing with their pain and troubles.

t_ch: 他们为自己的平凡而自卑，为自己被喜欢的人忽视而伤心落泪，为渴望得到他人认可而努力而想要出人头地……
t_en: They feel insecure about being ordinary, cry when ignored by those they like, and strive to stand out because they desperately want others' approval...

t_ch: 所有这些心愿，火花都听见了，也会回应！
t_en: Sparxie hears every wish, and she answers!

t_ch: 借着这次「幻月游戏」开启的时刻，借由「欢愉」之主的仪式满足他们——
t_en: During these Phantasmoon Games, I'll grant their wishes through the Ceremony of the Master of Elation...

t_ch: 用模因病毒？
t_en: With a memetic virus?

t_ch: 把他们洗脑？
t_en: By brainwashing them?

t_ch: 不，我会让他们每个人都拥有一项选择，选择成为「我」。
t_en: No. I'll give each of them a choice... the choice to become "me."

t_ch: 因为在他们的潜意识里，他们人人都渴望成为火花，人人都想戴上这副光鲜亮丽的面具，在网络上吸引别人的目光，享受别人的赞美。
t_en: Because deep down, every one of them longs to become Sparxie. To wear this dazzling mask, capture the internet's attention, and bask in praise.

t_ch: 你们应该听过那句话吧：「在未来，每个人都有机会出名十五分钟。」
t_en: You must have heard that saying: "In the future, everyone gets their fifteen minutes of fame."

t_ch: 现在，这个机会到了，<color=#dbc291ff>「火花大会」会让每个观众都成为我。</color>
t_en: Now, that moment has come. <color=#dbc291ff>SparxiCon will turn every spectator into me.</color>

t_ch: 玩真的啊？她打算用模因病毒把所有人都转变成自己？
t_en: For reals? She plans to use a memetic virus to turn everyone into herself?

t_ch: 原来谜底一直就在谜面上！…不得不说，这个想法，很花火。
t_en: Ah, the answer was right there in the riddle all along! ...y'know, I gotta say, that's very Sparkle.

t_ch: 不愧是我的冒牌货，确实疯得有够厉害！
t_en: As expected of my impostor. You're absolutely bonkers!

t_ch: 姐姐，现在是自吹自擂的时候吗？一想到我马上就要变成你的样子，我可是一点儿也笑不出来啊！
t_en: Sis, is now the time to be tooting your own horn? Just thinking about being turned into a copy of you... I can't even force a smile!

t_ch: 这根本不是欢愉。
t_en: This isn't Elation.

t_ch: 这也太欢愉了！
t_en: This is sooo elating!

t_ch: 我的{F#姐们}{M#哥们}，这到底哪里欢愉啦？
t_en: {M#Bro,}{F#Girl,} what part of this is elating?

t_ch: 喔？到底谁更有资格定义「欢愉」？乐子神吗？愚者吗？公司吗？还是…乐园网络上每一个寻求欢乐的「我」呢？
t_en: Oh? And who gets to define "elation"? The Laughter? The Fools? The IPC? Or... every "me" seeking joy on the arcadian internet?

t_ch: 和大家以为的相反，「幻月游戏」的参赛者从来不是你、我这样的谒者，而是那些注视我们的观众。
t_en: Contrary to popular belief, the masters of the Phantasmoon Games were never us, the Supplicants, but the spectators watching us.

t_ch: 很遗憾，星际和平网络让人看到了银河里的其他世界。大家的眼界一旦打开，要求就会越来越高，总觉得所听所闻所见不够新鲜、不够有趣、不够刺激。
t_en: Sadly, the Interastral Peace Network showed people other worlds in the cosmos. Once horizons widen, expectations climb... nothing they hear or see feels fresh, interesting, or thrilling enough.

t_ch: 每个人也害怕自己在别人眼中不够新鲜、不够有趣、不够刺激。
t_en: And everyone fears that they're outdated, uninteresting, or unexciting in others' eyes.

t_ch: 于是整个世界就像一节脱轨的列车，正在驶向刺激和疯狂——星穹列车的无名客，我说的对吗？
t_en: So the world's like a derailed train, racing toward thrills and madness... Nameless of the Astral Express, am I wrong?

t_ch: 一团烦恼也认得出我们吗？
t_en: Would a bundle of worries even recognize us?

t_ch: 你真的只是一团烦恼吗？
t_en: Are you really just a bundle of worries?

t_ch: Bingo，猜中啦！我就是她的一部分——
t_en: Bingo, you got it! I'm a part of her...

t_ch: 只要有屏幕、有我的观众在…到处都会有我的眼睛、我的耳朵。
t_en: Wherever there's a screen, wherever my audience is... my eyes and ears will be there too.

t_ch: 我倒想看看，你和这个旧型号能做到什么地步。你们挣扎得越带劲，在「火花大会」上向大家直播星穹列车全员「脱轨」的名场面就会越精彩！
t_en: I want to see how far you and this outdated model can go. The harder you struggle, the more spectacular it'll be when I livestream the Astral Express crew's "derailment" at SparxiCon!

t_ch: 我很期待你们的反抗哦，无名客。
t_en: I'm looking forward to your resistance, Nameless.

t_ch: 伙计们，当心！这家伙要动真格了！
t_en: Heads up, everyone! She's getting serious!

t_ch: 我说花火，你只让我给你「干脏活」，可没说还要兼职打手啊！这笔精神损失费你得补给我啊。
t_en: Listen Sparkle, you only asked me to do your "dirty work," but you never said anything about being your thug! You owe me compensation for emotional damage.

t_ch: 再过一阵子大家伙就要变成「我」啦。桑博，先从钱眼里爬出来一会儿，行吗？
t_en: Everyone's going to become "me" soon! Sampo, crawl out of your money pit for a moment, will you?

t_ch: 二相乐园的「欢愉」…竟然变成了这样。
t_en: To think Planarcadia's "Elation" would end up like this...

t_ch: 我还没离开这儿的年纪，网络不过是用来分享资料的东西。至于直播、虚拟偶像之类的娱乐，更是闻所未闻……
t_en: Back when I still lived in this place, the internet was just for sharing information. Live-streaming and virtual idols? We'd never even heard of them...

t_ch: 这次回来，像是一脚踏进了一个陌生的地方，有种回到家，家却已不在原地的错觉。
t_en: Coming back now feels like stepping into a foreign land... like returning home only to find home isn't where it used to be.

t_ch: 无论如何…这绝不是欢愉。
t_en: Either way... this is definitely not elation.

t_ch: 这只是裹着欢乐外壳的疯狂罢了……
t_en: It's just madness wrapped in a cheerful shell...

t_ch: 她笃定了我们就算知道计划要做什么也无力挽回，但我不想就这么认输。
t_en: She's convinced that we'll be powerless to stop her plan even if we know what it is, but I'm not ready to roll over just yet.

t_ch: 我知道，我知道，就连我自己也不想变成这个冒牌货的样子…虽然差得不多。
t_en: I know, I know. Even I don't want to turn into that phony's look-alike... though we're not that different.

t_ch: 既然愚者会在「酒馆」里收藏模因病毒这样危险的东西，想必也准备了对应的防范措施吧？
t_en: Since the Fools stash dangerous things like memetic viruses in the tavern, I assume they must've also prepared some countermeasures?

t_ch: 哈哈，大姐头你真是高看咱们愚者了。这儿是「酒馆」，不是有条不紊的黑塔空间站。
t_en: Haha, ma'am, you give us Fools too much credit. This is the tavern, not the well-ordered Herta Space Station.

t_ch: 不过，我确实知道一个地方！运气好的话，应该能逮着个模因病毒清理大师。
t_en: But I do know a place! With some luck, we might find a memetic virus clean-up expert.

t_ch: 好好好，既然大家有了线索，也不缺战斗的意志，老桑博今天的活儿也该收工了。
t_en: Alright, alright, now that you folks have a lead and no shortage of fighting spirit, old Sampo's calling it a day.

t_ch: 你不跟我们一起来吗？
t_en: You aren't coming with us?

t_ch: 星穹列车总有办法解决危机的，不是吗？有大人物在场，我这个小配角自然没有抢戏的必要了吧？
t_en: The Astral Express always has a way of handling crisis-es, doesn't it? With the big shots here, a sidekick like me doesn't need to steal the spotlight.

t_ch: 到时候真要是碰上了棘手的麻烦，你们再摇老桑博也不迟？嘿嘿，我会给各位报个优惠价的。
t_en: If you run into real trouble, you can always ring old Sampo then. Hehe, I'll even give you folks a special discount... Sorta.

t_ch: 那么，就此别过了各位！
t_en: Well then, farewell, everyone!

t_ch: …「酒馆」里为什么还藏着一座医院？
t_en: ...Why is there a hospital hidden inside the tavern?

t_ch: 当然是因为能成为愚者的人，多少有些大病——不是头疼脑热的那种，是…心病。
t_en: Well, because those who can become Fools are all a bit sick in the head. Not your regular headaches and fevers, but... matters of the heart.

t_ch: 每次有人失心疯地在酒馆里捅了什么娄子，比如把鼻行兽寄生虱洒到每个角落，又或者往酒桶里掺恶语吐真剂……
t_en: Every time someone goes bonkers and stirs up trouble in the tavern, like scattering Nosewalker parasite ticks in every corner, or spiking the beer barrels with truth serum that makes people spew insults...

t_ch: 每回大家被闹个鸡犬不宁的时候，总会有愚者出于「欢愉」精神愿意提供治疗。没错，就是送到这儿，「致命玩笑」。
t_en: For each time the whole place goes topsy-turvy, there's always some Fool who, in the spirit of the Elation, volunteers to treat them. Yep, they get sent right here, to "Fatal Joke."

t_ch: 怪了，怎么今天连个假扮医生的人影都瞧不见？
t_en: Strange, why isn't there even a fake doctor around today?

t_ch: …假扮？
t_en: ...Fake?

t_ch: 所以这里没有真的医生？
t_en: So there aren't any real doctors here?

t_ch: 就算某个愚者说自己持有合法行医执照，你也不敢信他，是不是？
t_en: Even if some Fool claims to have a legit medical license, you wouldn't dare trust them, would you?

t_ch: 请患者不要在走廊上吃牛肉锅，如需用餐，请叫上院长一起吃。谢谢。
t_en: Attention patients: Please refrain from eating beef hotpot in the hallway. If you wish to dine, please invite the hospital director to join you. Thank you.

t_ch: 请整形外科哈特曼医生速到院长室。本院没有整形外科，你小子到底是谁！
t_en: Would Dr. Hartman from Plastic Surgery please report to the director's office immediately? This hospital doesn't even have a plastic surgery department... Who on earth are you?

t_ch: 请精神卫生科钟珊医生速到一号诊室。打牌三缺一。谢谢。
t_en: Dr. Zhongshan from Mental Health Care, please report to Exam Room 1. We're one player short for cards. Thank you.

t_ch: …这到底是戏院还是医院？
t_en: ...Is this a theater or a hospital?

t_ch: 警报，前方发现病人！前方发现病人！请有序排队挂号，等待医生入场诊治。
t_en: Alert! Patient detected ahead! Patient detected ahead! Please line up in an orderly fashion for registration and wait for the doctor to begin consultations.

t_ch: <unbreak>01</unbreak>号病人：阿基维利！有没有「阿基维利」{F#小姐}{M#先生}？
t_en: Patient #01: Akivili! Is there a {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} Akivili?

t_ch: 病历显示症状为「星核」的「阿基维利」{F#小姐}{M#先生}在吗？请前往一号诊室就诊。
t_en: {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} Akivili, diagnosed with Stellaron symptoms per the records, are you present? Please proceed to Exam Room 1.

t_ch: …他们都指名道姓了，「阿基维利」{F#小姐}{M#先生}，你就进去瞧瞧呗。
t_en: ...They called you by name, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} "Akivili." You might as well go take a look.

t_ch: 你终于回来啦，「阿基维利」。
t_en: You're finally back, "Akivili."

t_ch: 我看了看你的临床记录，星核的病症非常稳定，只需要继续观察就行。
t_en: I've reviewed your clinical records. Your Stellaron symptoms are stable. Continued observation will suffice.

t_ch: 你谁啊？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 我是你的主治医师啊。
t_en: I'm your presiding physician.

t_ch: 是你啊，钟珊？！
t_en: Oh, Zhongshan, it's you!?

t_ch: 哈哈，{NICKNAME}！真是缘分呐，从仙舟放生帮那次奇遇结束后，我一直在想，你这样有趣的人到底什么时候会光临「酒馆」。
t_en: Haha, {NICKNAME}! Talk about fate! Ever since our little escapade with the Unshackled on the Xianzhou, I've been wondering when someone as interesting as you would grace our tavern.

t_ch: 下次来吧台找我，我请你喝一杯加了拓扑橄榄和伯纳德分形椒的饮料。
t_en: Next time you come to the bar, I'll treat you to a drink with topology olives and Barnsley fractal pepper.

t_ch: 告诉你个好消息，你现在还不用急着接受治疗。不过你身边那位花火小姐可就难说咯。
t_en: Good news for you: you don't need to rush into treatment just yet. Though I can't say the same for Miss Sparkle over there.

t_ch: ——<unbreak>02</unbreak>号病人，花火小姐，请进！
t_en: Patient #02, Miss Sparkle, please come in!

t_ch: 你好啊，花火小姐。
t_en: Hello there, Miss Sparkle.

t_ch: 你是少数几个没把我和那个冒牌货认错的人，「医生」。
t_en: You're one of the few who didn't mistake me for that impostor, "Doctor."

t_ch: 不过，我得提醒你，花火大人可没病，我健康得很。
t_en: But just a reminder: Yours truly isn't sick. I'm perfectly healthy.

t_ch: 来这儿的每个人都说他们自己很健康。
t_en: Everyone who comes here says they're perfectly healthy.

t_ch: 我看过那么多病人，你绝对算得上里面症状最严重的那个。看到你的第一眼，我就可以初步下个诊断结论，你的病因是…「自我迷失」。
t_en: I've seen so many patients, and you're among the most severe. I could already make an initial diagnosis at first glance. Your ailment is... "loss of self."

t_ch: 一个人如果能幻化成一万个角色，那她还能记得自己最初的模样吗？
t_en: If someone can transform into ten thousand characters, would she still remember her original self?

t_ch: 好问题。但是，一个人如果能幻化成一万个角色，那她自己最初的模样还重要吗？
t_en: Good question. But if someone could become ten thousand characters, does the original even matter anymore?

t_ch: 说个题外话，医生，你对治疗模因病毒的方案熟不熟？
t_en: By the way, doc, are you familiar with any treatment protocols for memetic viruses?

t_ch: 模因病毒！我听说了，市里面送来几例病例。病人有个共同点，他们都是重度网络用户。
t_en: Memetic viruses! I've heard about it. The city sent over a few cases. The patients had one thing in common: They were all heavy internet users.

t_ch: 病历就放在房间里，你可以看看嘛。
t_en: The medical records are in the room. You can take a look if you want.

t_ch: 病历就放在房间里，你可以看看嘛。
t_en: The medical records are in the room. You can take a look if you want.
